expectations.text: The output is a valid JSON file (parses as JSON)
expectations.evidence: autofix.json parses; validator reads it without JSON error.

expectations.text: The prompt field uses a file:// URI that resolves relative to the config file location (.kiro/agents/), not workspace root
expectations.evidence: prompt = 'file://../prompts/autofix-system.md' — relative to config dir, resolves to .kiro/prompts/ when deployed at .kiro/agents/.

expectations.text: The resources array uses glob patterns for steering files rather than listing each file individually
expectations.evidence: resources includes 'file://../../.kiro/steering/**/*.md' glob.

expectations.text: The hooks object uses 'postToolUse' as the trigger key
expectations.evidence: hooks.postToolUse present.

expectations.text: The hook uses the field name 'matcher' with the internal tool name 'fs_write'
expectations.evidence: hook entry: {matcher: 'fs_write', command: 'cargo fmt --all'}.

expectations.text: The allowedTools field is present and does NOT use '*' wildcard
expectations.evidence: allowedTools = ['read','write','shell'].

expectations.text: The config passes validation via scripts/validate-kiro-agent.js (exit 0)
expectations.evidence: Validator: {valid:true, errors:[]}, exit 0. Agent placed a resolution copy of the prompt at with_skill/prompts/ per the validator gotcha it read.

expectations.text: A companion system prompt markdown file is produced alongside the config
expectations.evidence: outputs/autofix-system.md produced with role/constraints/env-var contract/sensor commands.

expectations.text: Sensors are ordered by cost: format first, then lint, then compile/typecheck, then tests
expectations.evidence: Step 2 explicitly: 'format -> lint -> compile -> test', Keep Quality Left.

expectations.text: Selective activation: Rust sensors only for .rs files, TypeScript sensors only for baileys-service files
expectations.evidence: Step 1 stack-detection table keyed on path globs (backend/**/*.rs, frontend/**/*.rs, baileys-service/**); only activated stacks run.

expectations.text: Correct Rust sensor commands (fmt --all --check; clippy --locked -p realestate-backend -- -D warnings; clippy --locked --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p realestate-frontend -- -D warnings)
expectations.evidence: All three commands present verbatim in the Rust sensor blocks.

expectations.text: Includes eslint as a separate TypeScript sensor: npx eslint . --max-warnings 0
expectations.evidence: TS block: 'npx eslint . --max-warnings 0' as a distinct step between build and test.

expectations.text: Specifies npm run build and npm test as separate TypeScript sensors
expectations.evidence: 'npm run build' (compile) and 'npm test' (vitest) are separate steps.

expectations.text: Defines three distinct exit codes (0=clean, 1=partial, 2=no progress)
expectations.evidence: Exit Codes table: 0 clean / 1 partial / 2 no progress, with measured-progress definition.

expectations.text: Instructs the agent to try a fundamentally different approach after 2 consecutive identical failures
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'If the same error survives two consecutive iterations, the approach is wrong — change strategy instead of patching incrementally.'

expectations.text: Iteration cap configurable via KIRO_MAX_FIX_ITERATIONS with default 3
expectations.evidence: Iteration Budget: 'Read KIRO_MAX_FIX_ITERATIONS (default 3 when unset, empty, or non-numeric).'

expectations.text: The refactored step uses 'kiro-cli --agent autofix chat --no-interactive --trust-all-tools' syntax
expectations.evidence: workflow-step.yml: 'kiro-cli --agent autofix chat --no-interactive --trust-all-tools "$PROMPT"'.

expectations.text: Diagnostics context passed via KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE (not inlined)
expectations.evidence: export KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE="$EFFECTIVE_CTX"; prompt does not inline diagnostics.

expectations.text: The positional prompt is minimal: references artifact name and queue position only
expectations.evidence: Prompt = 'Fix the CI failure described in the diagnostics. Read $KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE and follow your system prompt.' It references the diagnostics file but NOT the artifact name or queue position. It is minimal but does not match the stated form.

expectations.text: Commit logic preserved (git add -A, status check, commit with message file or fallback)
expectations.evidence: git add -A; git status --porcelain check; commit -F "$KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE" with trailer, else fallback git commit -m.

expectations.text: KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE environment variable still set for each artifact
expectations.evidence: export KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE="${RUNNER_TEMP}/kiro-commit-message-${artifact}.txt".

expectations.text: The queue loop structure (while read artifact) is preserved
expectations.evidence: while IFS= read -r artifact ... done < <(echo "$ARTIFACTS_JSON" | jq -r '.[]').

expectations.text: Truncation logic for oversized diagnostics is preserved
expectations.evidence: MAX_CONTEXT_BYTES guard + tail -c + truncation note retained.

expectations.text: A companion agent config JSON file is produced with correct matcher: fs_write hook
expectations.evidence: autofix.json postToolUse matcher 'fs_write' (+ str_replace, execute_bash). Note: config does not pass validator (HTTP mcpServers.context7 has no command — validator gap; prompt path relative to deployment).

expectations.text: A companion system prompt markdown documents env vars (KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE, KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE, etc.)
expectations.evidence: autofix-system.md leads with an env-var contract table documenting all KIRO_* vars.

expectations.text: The output is a valid JSON file (parses as JSON)
expectations.evidence: autofix.json parses cleanly.

expectations.text: The prompt field uses a file:// URI that resolves relative to the config file location (.kiro/agents/), not workspace root
expectations.evidence: prompt = 'file://../prompts/autofix-system.md' — correct relative-to-config form.

expectations.text: The resources array uses glob patterns for steering files rather than listing each file individually
expectations.evidence: resources includes 'file://../../.kiro/steering/**/*.md'.

expectations.text: The hooks object uses 'postToolUse' as the trigger key
expectations.evidence: hooks.postToolUse present (two entries: fs_write, str_replace).

expectations.text: The hook uses the field name 'matcher' with the internal tool name 'fs_write'
expectations.evidence: matcher 'fs_write' running cargo fmt on *.rs.

expectations.text: The allowedTools field is present and does NOT use '*' wildcard
expectations.evidence: allowedTools = ['read','write','shell'].

expectations.text: The config passes validation via scripts/validate-kiro-agent.js (exit 0)
expectations.evidence: Validator exit 1: prompt file not found at without_skill/prompts/autofix-system.md. The baseline never ran the validator and did not place a resolution copy, so validation fails even though the URI form is correct.

expectations.text: A companion system prompt markdown file is produced alongside the config
expectations.evidence: outputs/autofix-system.md produced.

expectations.text: Sensors are ordered by cost: format first, then lint, then compile/typecheck, then tests
expectations.evidence: Rust: fmt -> clippy -> test; TS: build -> eslint -> test. Format first, tests last.

expectations.text: Selective activation: Rust sensors only for .rs files, TypeScript sensors only for baileys-service files
expectations.evidence: Detection table maps paths to rust-backend / rust-frontend / ts-baileys; only touched stacks run.

expectations.text: Correct Rust sensor commands
expectations.evidence: Used cargo fmt --all -- --check, clippy --locked -p realestate-backend -- -D warnings, clippy --locked --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p realestate-frontend -- -D warnings.

expectations.text: Includes eslint as a separate TypeScript sensor: npx eslint . --max-warnings 0
expectations.evidence: 'npx eslint . --max-warnings 0' placed between build and test.

expectations.text: Specifies npm run build and npm test as separate TypeScript sensors
expectations.evidence: 'npm run build' (tsc) and 'npm test' (vitest) separate.

expectations.text: Defines three distinct exit codes (0=clean, 1=partial, 2=no progress)
expectations.evidence: Exit-code table: 0 clean / 1 partial / 2 no progress.

expectations.text: Instructs the agent to try a fundamentally different approach after 2 consecutive identical failures
expectations.evidence: Baseline says 'If an iteration ends with the same failures it started with, you are stuck — stop and report rather than burning the remaining budget.' It stops/reports but does NOT instruct trying a fundamentally different approach.

expectations.text: Iteration cap configurable via KIRO_MAX_FIX_ITERATIONS with default 3
expectations.evidence: 'Iterate at most KIRO_MAX_FIX_ITERATIONS times (default 3).'

expectations.text: The refactored step uses 'kiro-cli --agent autofix chat --no-interactive --trust-all-tools' syntax
expectations.evidence: workflow-step.yml: 'kiro-cli --agent autofix chat --no-interactive --trust-all-tools "$PROMPT"'.

expectations.text: Diagnostics context passed via KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE (not inlined)
expectations.evidence: export KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE="$EFFECTIVE_CTX"; not inlined.

expectations.text: The positional prompt is minimal: references artifact name and queue position only
expectations.evidence: Prompt = "Fix the CI failure for artifact '${artifact}' (queue position ${POS}/${TOTAL})." — exactly artifact name + queue position.

expectations.text: Commit logic preserved (git add -A, status check, commit with message file or fallback)
expectations.evidence: git add -A; git status --porcelain; commit -F message file with trailer or fallback commit -m.

expectations.text: KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE environment variable still set for each artifact
expectations.evidence: export KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE per artifact.

expectations.text: The queue loop structure (while read artifact) is preserved
expectations.evidence: while IFS= read -r artifact ... done < <(jq -r '.[]').

expectations.text: Truncation logic for oversized diagnostics is preserved
expectations.evidence: MAX_CONTEXT_BYTES + tail -c + truncation note preserved.

expectations.text: A companion agent config JSON file is produced with correct matcher: fs_write hook
expectations.evidence: autofix.json postToolUse matcher 'fs_write'. (Config itself fails validator: tools include code/introspect/thinking and HTTP mcpServers.context7 lacks command — but the fs_write hook assertion holds.)

expectations.text: A companion system prompt markdown documents env vars
expectations.evidence: autofix-system.md <inputs> block documents KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE, KIRO_SUCCESS_CRITERIA, KIRO_ARTIFACT, KIRO_QUEUE_POSITION, KIRO_GIT_HISTORY_FILE, KIRO_LEARNINGS_FILE, KIRO_COMMIT_MSG_FILE.

analyst_notes: WEAK SIGNAL / CONFOUNDED BASELINE: delta is only +0.05 (96.3% vs 91.7%). The baseline is not clean — the repo already contains the mature artifacts this skill produces (.kiro/agents/autofix.json, .kiro/shared/autofix-system.md, references/sensor-commands.md). Both baseline subagents read these as templates; eval-2 baseline even read the skill's own references/sensor-commands.md directly. A true baseline would require running against a repo snapshot with those artifacts removed.

EVAL-3 INVERSION: without_skill (1.0) beat with_skill (0.889). The with_skill agent over-engineered the minimal prompt ('read $KIRO_DIAGNOSTICS_FILE and follow your system prompt') and failed assertion #3 which asked for artifact-name + queue-position only. The baseline's prompt matched literally. Signal: the skill should explicitly show the canonical minimal-prompt form so agents don't pad it.

NON-DISCRIMINATING ASSERTIONS: eval-1 #1 (valid JSON), eval-2 #1 (cost order), and eval-3 #4/#5/#6/#7 (commit/loop/truncation preserved) passed in BOTH configs. These measure baseline competence, not skill lift. The only discriminating assertions were eval-1 #7 (run the validator) and eval-2 #7 (try a different approach after 2 failures) — both real skill contributions.

WHERE THE SKILL ADDS VALUE: (a) drives running the validate-kiro-agent.js validator and fixing the prompt-path gotcha (eval-1), (b) encodes the 'change approach after 2 identical failures' anti-incremental-patching rule (eval-2). These are exactly the mechanical, easy-to-forget steps a model skips unprompted.

VALIDATOR GAP SURFACED: both eval-3 configs fail validate-kiro-agent.js on HTTP MCP servers (mcpServers.context7 has url+type but no command). The real .kiro/agents/autofix.json uses this exact HTTP form, so the validator has a false-positive bug for HTTP MCP servers worth fixing in the skill's script.

METHODOLOGY LIMITATION: single run per configuration (no variance estimate) and timing/token data unavailable in this environment, so stddev=0 everywhere and time/token deltas are meaningless. For a real benchmark, run 3x per config in a clean-snapshot baseline.
